steps.title: Spot the contradiction to the hypothesis
steps.content: The hypothesis claims the association exists only in undisturbed waters (based on Guédron's wastewater-disturbed findings showing no association). But Tjerngren found the association in urban-development-disturbed waters, contradicting the hypothesis.

steps.title: Interpret the logical consequence
steps.content:
Since different disturbances produce different results on the association—wastewater breaks it; urban development preserves it—Guédron's findings don't represent all disturbances.

A directly states this: wastewater's effects shouldn't be taken as indicative of all human disturbances.

The other three either invent methodological differences (B), make unsupported claims about scale (C), or misread what levels were compared (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
